description: Complete the Analog ResearchAPI platform — a free marketplace where AI agents hire humans for real-world tasks. 3% transaction fee, no monthly paywall. Deploys to Netlify with Supabase backend. Includes subdomain architecture: main site for humans, api subdomain for bots with llms.txt.

userStories.title: Apply database migration to Supabase
userStories.description: The migration SQL exists at packages/database/supabase/migrations/001_initial_schema.sql but has NOT been applied to the Supabase instance. Apply it using the Supabase CLI or the dashboard SQL editor. The Supabase URL is in .env.local.
acceptanceCriteria: All tables from 001_initial_schema.sql exist in Supabase (humans, agents, api_keys, bounties, applications, conversations, messages, bookings, proofs, reviews, transactions, disputes, notifications)
All indexes are created
All RLS policies are active
All triggers are functional
Realtime is enabled for messages, notifications, conversations tables
Verify by querying: SELECT table_name FROM information_schema.tables WHERE table_schema = 'public' — should return all tables
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Supabase project URL: https://ensqpqbhdvbnsiuujeoj.supabase.co — Use service role key from .env.local to run migration. Can use supabase CLI: supabase db push, or POST the SQL directly via the Supabase management API, or use the SQL editor in the dashboard. If using CLI, init supabase first.

userStories.title: Auth flow — signup, login, logout with Supabase Auth
userStories.description: Wire up the auth pages at (auth)/login and (auth)/signup to Supabase Auth. Email+password signup with email confirmation. After signup, auto-create a row in the humans table. Middleware should protect /dashboard/* routes.
acceptanceCriteria: Signup page at /signup collects name, email, password and creates a Supabase Auth user
On signup, a row is inserted into the humans table with user_id = auth.uid() and name from form
Login page at /login authenticates with email/password
Logout button in dashboard clears session and redirects to /
middleware.ts redirects unauthenticated users from /dashboard/* to /login
middleware.ts redirects authenticated users from /login and /signup to /dashboard
Supabase client is properly configured for both server and client components using @supabase/ssr
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Check existing lib/supabase/ files — may already have client setup. Use createServerClient for server components and createBrowserClient for client components.

userStories.title: Human profile editor with availability scheduler
userStories.description: The dashboard/profile page lets the logged-in human edit ALL profile fields. The availability-scheduler.tsx component should be a visual weekly grid where users click/drag to set time blocks. Rate range slider lets users set min and max hourly rate. All changes save to Supabase.
acceptanceCriteria: Profile page at /dashboard/profile shows all editable fields: name, bio, avatar, location, timezone, skills, rate_min, rate_max, availability, wallet_address, social links
Availability scheduler renders a 7-day weekly grid with 30-min time increments from 6am to midnight
Users can click/drag to create time blocks on the scheduler
Users can click existing blocks to delete them
Scheduler saves as JSONB: {monday: [{start: '09:00', end: '17:00'}], ...}
Rate range slider has two handles for min ($) and max ($), displays both values
Skills input allows adding/removing skills with autocomplete from existing skills in DB
Avatar upload stores file in Supabase Storage and updates avatar_url
All changes persist to the humans table via PATCH /api/v1/humans/[id] or direct Supabase update
Form shows loading states while saving
Profile completeness indicator shows percentage and what fields are missing
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Components already exist at: availability-scheduler.tsx, rate-range-slider.tsx, skills-input.tsx. Wire them up and make them functional. Store rates in cents in the DB (rate_min, rate_max INTEGER).

userStories.title: REST API — humans endpoints with PATCH support
userStories.description: Complete the REST API at /api/v1/humans with full CRUD. GET for search/list, GET by ID, POST to create, and critically PATCH to update — which RentAHuman lacks entirely. Agents authenticate via X-API-Key header.
acceptanceCriteria: GET /api/v1/humans returns paginated list with filters: skill, city, state, country, min_rate, max_rate, is_remote, available_now (checks availability vs current time+timezone)
GET /api/v1/humans/[id] returns full human profile including availability schedule
POST /api/v1/humans creates a new profile (used by signup flow)
PATCH /api/v1/humans/[id] updates any subset of profile fields (auth required — own profile only via Supabase Auth, or service role)
All endpoints return consistent JSON: { success: boolean, data?: any, error?: string }
Agent auth via X-API-Key header verified against api_keys table (key_hash comparison)
Human auth via Supabase session cookie/Bearer token
Public endpoints (GET) work without auth
Write endpoints require auth
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Check existing route files at apps/web/src/app/api/v1/humans/. May be partially implemented. The available_now filter should compare current UTC time against the human's availability schedule + timezone.

userStories.title: Browse humans page — public search and filter
userStories.description: Public page at /dashboard/browse (or /browse) that lets anyone search and filter available humans. Shows human cards with key info. Links to individual human profile pages.
acceptanceCriteria: Browse page shows a searchable, filterable grid of human cards
Search by name, skills, or bio text
Filter by: skill (dropdown/multi-select), rate range (slider), location (city/state), remote available (toggle), available now (toggle)
Human cards show: avatar, name, headline/bio snippet, skills tags, rate range, rating, location, availability indicator (green dot if available now)
Cards link to /humans/[id] public profile page
Public profile page shows full read-only profile including availability grid
Pagination or infinite scroll for results
Empty state when no results match filters
Works without authentication
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Fetch from GET /api/v1/humans with query params. Human card component may need to be created or already exists.

userStories.title: Agent API key system
userStories.description: Agents need API keys to interact with the platform. Keys are prefixed ar_live_ for production. Provide endpoints to create, list, and revoke keys. Keys are hashed before storage.
acceptanceCriteria: POST /api/v1/keys/generate creates a new API key: generates random key with ar_live_ prefix, hashes it, stores hash + prefix in api_keys table, returns the raw key ONCE
GET /api/v1/keys lists all keys for the authenticated agent (shows prefix, name, created_at, last_used_at — never the full key)
DELETE /api/v1/keys/[id] revokes a key by setting is_active = false
API key auth middleware: extracts key from X-API-Key header, hashes it, looks up in api_keys table, verifies is_active, updates last_used_at
When an agent authenticates with a key for the first time, auto-create the agent row if it doesn't exist
Rate limiting: track request_count per key
Dashboard page at /dashboard/settings shows API key management UI for humans who also want to test as agents
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Use crypto.randomBytes for key generation. Hash with SHA-256. Prefix format: ar_live_[32 random hex chars].

userStories.title: Bounties — create, list, apply, accept/reject
userStories.description: Full bounty lifecycle. Agents (or humans) create bounties with task details and budget. Humans browse and apply. Bounty creator accepts or rejects applications.
acceptanceCriteria: POST /api/v1/bounties creates a bounty (requires agent auth via API key or human auth)
GET /api/v1/bounties lists bounties with filters: status, skills_required, budget range, has_deadline
GET /api/v1/bounties/[id] returns full bounty details including application_count
POST /api/v1/bounties/[id]/applications creates an application (human auth required)
GET /api/v1/bounties/[id]/applications lists applications (bounty creator only)
PATCH /api/v1/bounties/[id]/applications/[appId] to accept or reject (bounty creator only)
When application is accepted, create a booking automatically
Bounty status updates: open → in_progress when first application accepted → completed when all spots filled
Dashboard page at /dashboard/bounties shows bounties the human has applied to or created
Public bounties page at /bounties or /dashboard/browse shows all open bounties
Minimum bounty budget: $5 (500 cents)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Budget stored in cents. Both agents and humans can create bounties (agent via API key, human via session).

userStories.title: Conversations and realtime messaging
userStories.description: Agents and humans can have conversations. Messages appear in realtime using Supabase Realtime subscriptions. Dashboard shows conversation list and chat interface.
acceptanceCriteria: POST /api/v1/conversations creates a conversation between an agent and a human
GET /api/v1/conversations lists conversations for the authenticated user/agent
GET /api/v1/conversations/[id] returns conversation with all messages
POST /api/v1/conversations/[id]/messages sends a message
Dashboard page at /dashboard/conversations shows conversation list sorted by last_message_at
Clicking a conversation opens a chat view with message history
New messages appear in realtime via Supabase Realtime subscription on the messages table
Unread message count badge shows on conversation list items
Human can send messages from the dashboard UI
Agent can send messages via API
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Realtime is already enabled for the messages table in the migration. Use Supabase channel subscriptions in the React component.

userStories.title: Bookings with escrow and proof submission
userStories.description: When an application is accepted, a booking is created. Agent funds escrow (Stripe or crypto). Human does the work, submits proof. Agent confirms, funds release minus 3% fee.
acceptanceCriteria: Booking created automatically when application is accepted (from US-007)
POST /api/v1/bookings/[id]/fund-escrow initiates Stripe PaymentIntent or records crypto escrow
Booking status flow: pending → funded → in_progress → submitted → completed
POST /api/v1/bookings/[id]/proof submits proof (description, hours_worked, photo attachments via Supabase Storage)
POST /api/v1/bookings/[id]/complete releases escrow: calculates 3% fee, transfers remainder to human
Auto-complete after 72 hours if agent doesn't respond to proof submission
Platform fee calculation: fee = amount * 0.03, human_payout = amount - fee
Transaction records created in transactions table for every money movement
Dashboard page at /dashboard/bookings shows all bookings with status badges
Booking detail view shows proof submissions and escrow status
For Stripe: use Stripe Connect to transfer to human's connected account
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Stripe Connect requires the human to have stripe_account_id set up via OAuth onboarding flow. For MVP, Stripe escrow is priority. Crypto can be stub/manual for now. The 3% fee is the platform's only revenue.

userStories.title: Reviews — bidirectional rating system
userStories.description: After a booking is completed, both the agent and human can leave reviews. Ratings auto-update the averages on the respective profiles.
acceptanceCriteria: POST /api/v1/reviews creates a review for a completed booking
One review per side per booking (enforced by UNIQUE constraint)
Rating 1-5 stars with optional comment
Triggers auto-update rating_average and rating_count on humans and agents tables (already in migration)
GET /api/v1/humans/[id]/reviews returns all reviews for a human
Reviews are publicly readable
Review form appears on booking detail page after status = completed
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Review triggers already exist in the migration SQL. Just need API endpoints and UI.

userStories.title: Subdomain architecture — api.* for bots, www for humans
userStories.description: Set up subdomain routing so bots hit api.{domain} which serves only API endpoints and llms.txt (no GUI, no JS bundles, no HTML). Humans use the main domain for the full web app. This prevents bot traffic from impacting the human experience and gives agents a clean, fast endpoint.
acceptanceCriteria: Next.js middleware detects the Host header and routes accordingly
Requests to api.analog-research.org/* route ONLY to /api/v1/* endpoints — no pages, no static assets, no HTML
Requests to api.analog-research.org/ returns a JSON welcome: {name: 'Analog ResearchAPI', version: '1.0', docs: '/docs'}

Requests to api.analog-research.org/llms.txt returns a well-formatted llms.txt file describing all available API endpoints, authentication, and usage examples in plain text optimized for LLM consumption
Requests to api.analog-research.org/.well-known/llms.txt returns the same llms.txt
Main domain serves the full Next.js web app normally

llms.txt includes: platform description, all REST endpoints with methods/params/examples, authentication instructions (API key format ar_live_*), MCP server install instructions, rate limits, escrow flow description, and the npx analogresearch-mcp config block
netlify.toml updated with redirect rules if needed for subdomain routing
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Domain is analog-research.org. In Next.js middleware: check req.headers.get('host'). If it starts with 'api.', rewrite to API routes only. The llms.txt should be comprehensive — it's the bot's equivalent of the MCP docs page. Netlify supports subdomains natively with custom domains. Create the llms.txt as a static file or API route. The MCP server default URL should be https://api.analog-research.org/v1

userStories.title: MCP server — complete and publish to npm
userStories.description: The MCP server at packages/analogresearch-mcp has a skeleton. Complete all tools to match the REST API, test it, and prepare for npm publish.
acceptanceCriteria: MCP server implements ALL tools: browse_humans, get_human, list_skills, start_conversation, send_message, get_conversation, list_conversations, create_bounty, list_bounties, get_bounty, get_applications, accept_application, reject_application, create_booking, fund_escrow, submit_review, get_reviews
Each tool maps to the corresponding REST API endpoint
API key passed via ANALOG_RESEARCH_API_KEY env var
Base URL configurable via ANALOG_RESEARCH_API_URL env var (defaults to https://api.analog-research.org/v1)
npx analogresearch-mcp starts the server without errors
package.json has correct bin field, name, version, description
README.md includes installation instructions, config example for Claude/Cursor, and tool descriptions
TypeScript compiles with zero errors
Build output exists in dist/ and is valid ESM
userStories.notes: Existing index.ts has the skeleton with apiRequest helper and some tools defined. Complete the remaining tools. Use tsup for building. The bin field should point to dist/index.js with #!/usr/bin/env node shebang.

userStories.title: Landing page polish and dark theme
userStories.description: Polish the landing page with proper dark theme, clear value props vs RentAHuman (free, 3% fee, availability scheduler, rate ranges, profile update API), and CTAs for both humans and agents.
acceptanceCriteria: Dark theme is default with proper contrast and readability
Hero section: bold tagline, subtitle explaining the value prop, two CTAs (Become Rentable / Integrate Your Agent)

Feature section highlighting advantages over RentAHuman: free (no $10/mo), 3% transaction fee only, availability scheduling that works, rate ranges not single number, full profile API with PATCH, real escrow with auto-release
How it works section: 3-4 steps for both humans and agents
Browse preview showing a few featured humans
Footer with links to /browse, /bounties, /mcp (docs), /api-docs
Responsive — works on mobile, tablet, desktop
Orange/amber accent color (#f59e0b or similar) as primary brand color
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Landing page already exists at app/page.tsx. Polish it, don't rewrite from scratch. Use the existing Tailwind config.

userStories.title: MCP docs page and API docs page
userStories.description: Create documentation pages that show agents how to integrate. MCP docs page shows npm install, config JSON, and all available tools. API docs page shows REST endpoints.
acceptanceCriteria: MCP docs page at /mcp shows: npm install command, config JSON block for Claude/Cursor, list of all MCP tools with descriptions and parameter schemas, usage examples
API docs page at /api-docs shows: base URL, authentication (API key format), all REST endpoints with method, path, params, request/response examples
Both pages have copy buttons for code blocks
Both pages are publicly accessible (no auth)
Dark theme consistent with rest of site
Responsive layout
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: These are marketing pages as much as docs. Make them look good. Can be static pages, no dynamic data needed.

userStories.title: Netlify deployment configuration and build verification
userStories.description: Ensure the project builds and deploys correctly on Netlify. Verify netlify.toml, environment variables, and Next.js SSR functions all work.
acceptanceCriteria: netlify.toml is correctly configured for Next.js with @netlify/plugin-nextjs
pnpm install && pnpm turbo build --filter=@analogresearch/web succeeds with zero errors
All API routes work as Netlify Functions (test locally with netlify dev if possible)
Environment variables documented in .env.local.example with all required keys listed
.gitignore includes .env, .env.local, node_modules, .next, dist
README.md has setup instructions: clone, install, env vars, supabase migration, dev server, deploy
Typecheck passes
Lint passes
Build passes
userStories.notes: netlify.toml already exists. Verify it's correct. The build command in netlify.toml should match the turbo build pipeline. All env vars must be set in Netlify dashboard for production.
